newsSince: The June 8 Capital Markets Day is already in the June 9 case. New since: a Heidelberg Materials order to deploy autonomous driverless haul trucks at an Australian quarry. However, CMD coverage emphasized SLOWING automation adoption (3,900 driverless machines vs 3,100 in 2024, only +26%, decelerating) and reduced mining-house capex appetite — a mild offset to the bullish prior framing.

sources.label: International Mining: Epiroc CMD outlines automation/electrification advances

verdicts.rankRationale: The June 8 CMD that drove the prior 'up' case is now stale; nothing new since June 9 and Q2 isn't until July 17. CMD recaps flag decelerating autonomy adoption (~26% machine growth), a mild qualitative softening of the AI-metals-productivity thesis. Hold rank 82.
verdicts.bull: 3,900 driverless machines + Ericsson 5G GTM and quarrying autonomy ride AI-driven metals/productivity demand.
verdicts.neutral: Quality automation franchise but adoption is decelerating and link to AI is indirect.
verdicts.bear: Mining capex caution and slowing autonomy uptake cap the latent-node upside; +25% YTD.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Mining-house capex pullback; decelerating autonomy adoption
